vocab.nonché: as well as; and also (formal connector)
vocab.vi invito a: I invite you to; I encourage you to

vocab.in falesia: on the cliff; at the crag (climbing context)

vocab.farsi male: to get hurt; to injure oneself

vocab.sia: both; as well as; is (subjunctive of 'essere')
vocab.mi ci metto pure io: I include myself too; I put myself in too

vocab.fin da piccoli: since they were little; from a young age
vocab.missini: Missini (members of the Italian Social Movement; context-specific)

vocab.non è sempre così: it's not always like that; that's not always the case

vocab.sotto ogni punto di vista: from every point of view; in every respect
vocab.basta pensare: just think; it is enough to think

vocab.non poco: quite a lot; not a little (idiom meaning 'a lot')

vocab.da secondo: from second (from then on; from that moment)
vocab.iniziavo a: I started to; I began to
vocab.scendere: to go down; to get off

vocab.da bambino: as a child; when I was a child
vocab.mi divertivo: I had fun; I enjoyed myself

vocab.è troppo grande: is too big; is too great
vocab.facevo di tutto: I did everything; I tried everything
vocab.pur di non: just to not; in order not to
vocab.il minimo possibile: the minimum possible; as little as possible
vocab.ero inoltre: I was also; furthermore I was
vocab.molto negato: very bad at; very unskilled at

vocab.noto: well-known; famous (note: here likely means 'well-known' climbing routes)
vocab.in falesia: on the crag; at the crag (outdoor climbing wall)
vocab.pur avendo: despite having; even though they had
vocab.quattro anni meno di me: four years less than me; four years younger than me

vocab.la corda tesa: the tight rope (climbing term: rope held tight for safety)
vocab.non esisteva: there was no; did not exist
vocab.molare: to let go; to give up

vocab.mi diceva: told me; used to tell me
vocab.delle via: of the route; of the way (note: 'via' in climbing = route)
vocab.a divertirmi: to have fun; to enjoy myself
vocab.ed ecco che: and here; and here is where

vocab.da piccolo: as a child; when (he) was little

vocab.non ne voleva proprio sapere: he really didn't want to know about it; he wanted nothing to do with it
vocab.prestare: to lend; to give (note: likely a mishearing or typo, contextually may mean 'for' or 'because of')
vocab.in compagnia di: in the company of; together with
vocab.che non per: rather than for; instead of for

vocab.sotto indicazione di: under the instruction of; under the guidance of
vocab.sciogliermi: to loosen myself; to relax myself
vocab.mi ero appeso: I had hung myself (reflexive, not literal suicide)
vocab.senza riuscire a: without managing to; without being able to

vocab.così facendo: by doing so; in this way
vocab.via di un grado molto più basso: route of a much lower grade
vocab.inizio a: I start to; I begin to

vocab.come già detto: as already said; as already mentioned

vocab.arrampicatori: climbers (note: refers to people who climb)
vocab.per un errore: because of a mistake; due to an error

vocab.non è stato concluso: was not finished; was not completed

vocab.ci fa: makes us; causes us (note: 'ci' = us, 'fa' = makes/does)
vocab.rannicchiarsi: to curl up; to huddle (note: reflexive verb)

vocab.sbattere contro: to crash into; to hit against
vocab.farsi: to hurt oneself; to get hurt

vocab.ed ecco: and here is; and here are
vocab.non dico: I'm not saying; I don't say
vocab.riuscire a: to manage to; to succeed in
vocab.gestirla: to manage it; to handle it ('la' = 'it', referring to fear)
vocab.farci male: to hurt ourselves; to get hurt ('ci' = 'ourselves')

vocab.ero disposto a tutto: I was willing to do anything

vocab.al terra: to the ground (note: should be 'a terra')

vocab.a mancarmi le forze: I was running out of strength; my strength was failing
vocab.che non sarei mai arrivato: that I would never arrive; that I would never make it

vocab.mi lasciavo: I let myself; I allowed myself
vocab.avevo allungato: I had extended; I had lengthened
vocab.anti: against (note: likely a mishearing or typo for 'a', meaning 'to')

vocab.imperiali: imperials (note: likely refers to 'imperial' things or people)
vocab.l'anno dopo: the year after; the next year
vocab.tomo: tome; volume (note: 'tomo' is a large book or volume)

vocab.avevo fatto: I had done; I had made

vocab.un grande deficit: a big deficit; a large lack
vocab.si è sicuri: one is sure; people are sure
vocab.di una via: on a route; of a route

vocab.fori: holes (note: likely 'fuori' = outside, but as written means 'holes')
vocab.non potevo: I couldn't; I was not able to
vocab.potevo apprende: I could learn (note: likely 'apprendere' = to learn)
vocab.non manca: does not lack; is not missing

vocab.mettermi in gioco: put myself on the line; get involved (idiom)
vocab.chiudere: to finish; to close (context: to complete something)

vocab.un certo punto: at a certain point; at some point
vocab.un tiro: a move; a shot (context: climbing move or attempt)
vocab.ti devi mettere in gioco: you have to put yourself on the line; you have to get involved (lit. 'put yourself in play')

vocab.la seconda svolta: the second turn; the second change
vocab.in fallisi nuove: on new falls (possible typo, likely 'falesie nuove' = new cliffs)

vocab.mettono: they put; they give (context: cause a feeling)
vocab.una certa: a certain; some (context: a degree of)
vocab.farsi assicurare: to get assured; to have oneself reassured

vocab.fare qualche metro in più: go a few meters more; do a bit more

vocab.il più possibile: as much as possible; as far as possible

vocab.quando si vuole: when one wants; when you want
vocab.che si prova: that one feels; that you feel

vocab.mettersi in gioco: to get involved; to challenge oneself

vocab.ringrazio moltissimo: thank very much; thank a lot
vocab.sia su: both on; whether on (used for listing multiple things)

vocab.che sia: whether it is; even if it is
vocab.ci vediamo: see you; we'll see each other
